revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:青侍]]
'''Blue Samurai''' (青侍) were {{ill|Samurai|en|Samurai|qid=Q38142|lt=samurai|1=Samurai}} who served in the {{ill|Royal household|en|Royal household|qid=Q17146103|lt=household administration|1=Royal household}} of {{ill|Nobility|en|Nobility|qid=Q134737|lt=aristocrats|1=Nobility}} and {{ill|Kuge|en|Kuge|qid=Q1196276|lt=Kuge|1=Kuge}} (court nobles). The term is considered synonymous with *namazamurai* as mentioned in {{ill|Ōkagami|en|Ōkagami|qid=Q385120|lt=Ōkagami|1=Ōkagami}} and appears in various historical diaries such as {{ill|Chūyūki|ja|中右記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chūyūki|qq=}} and {{ill|Meigetsuki|ja|明月記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meigetsuki|qq=}}, as well as in literary works like {{ill|Konjaku Monogatarishū|en|Konjaku Monogatarishū|qid=Q1507085|lt=Konjaku Monogatari|1=Konjaku Monogatarishū}}, {{ill|Kokon Chomonjū|en|Kokon Chomonjū|qid=Q6426772|lt=Kokon Chomonjū|1=Kokon Chomonjū}}, {{ill|The Tale of the Heike|en|The Tale of the Heike|qid=Q830740|lt=Heike Monogatari|1=The Tale of the Heike}}, and {{ill|Uji Shūi Monogatari|en|Uji Shūi Monogatari|qid=Q2446713|lt=Uji Shūi Monogatari|1=Uji Shūi Monogatari}}. == Overview == The term emerged during the appearance of the {{ill|samurai class|ja|侍階層|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/samurai class|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in the {{ill|11th century|en|11th century|qid=Q7063|lt=11th century|1=11th century}}, referring to those who served in the household administration of the kuge. They Ranked below {{ill|shodaibu|ja|諸大夫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shodaibu|1=shodaibu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11632069}}, who could be promoted to the fourth or fifth Ranks, and were typically placed in the sixth Rank (although some rare individuals attained the fifth Rank). Since the attire for the sixth Rank, {{ill|ihō|ja|位袍|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ihō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, was a blue-based color called {{ill|hanada|ja|縹|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/hanada|qq=}}, the term "Blue Samurai" was coined. The color blue also symbolized "immaturity," leading to the meaning of a young or lower-Ranked samurai. In the late medieval period, as the household administration system of the kuge, including the {{ill|Mandokoro|en|Mandokoro|qid=Q2279768|lt=Mandokoro|1=Mandokoro}} and {{ill|house stewards|ja|家司|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/house stewards|1=house stewards|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11455396}}, was dismantled, the retainer structure of the kuge was reorganized. The retainers were divided into two main groups: shodaibu and Blue Samurai, who made up the {{ill|household servants|ja|家僕|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/household servants|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 家来|1=household servants|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11455530}}. Lower- and middle-Ranking kuge who could not maintain shodaibu relied solely on Blue Samurai for household management, sometimes using the terms interchangeably (as seen in {{ill|Kyōgonkyōki|ja|教言卿記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyōgonkyōki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}). During the {{ill|Sengoku period|en|Sengoku period|qid=Q204023|lt=Sengoku period|1=Sengoku period}}, when issuing {{ill|hōsho|ja|奉書|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/hōsho|1=hōsho|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11445510}} (official orders) to lower-Ranking individuals, the {{ill|benkan|ja|弁官|1=benkan|lt=Benkan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/benkan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q3270768}} or other officials overseeing the process often ordered their Blue Samurai to draft and issue the document, a practice known as '''Blue Samurai hōsho''' (青侍奉書), which is referenced in sources like {{ill|Chikanaga Kyōki|ja|親長卿記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikanaga Kyōki|1=Chikanaga-kyō-ki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11630466}}, {{ill|Nobutane Kyōki|ja|宣胤卿記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nobutane Kyōki|1=Nobutane-kyō-ki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11452953}}, and {{ill|Jitsuryū Kōki|ja|実隆公記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jitsuryū Kōki|1=Sanetaka-kō Ki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11452887}}. Additionally, during the late medieval period, household servants played a role in the military power of the kuge, contributing to the maintenance of {{ill|Manorialism|en|Manorialism|qid=Q1550557|lt=shōen|1=Manorialism}} control.<ref>Motoki Kuroda, "The Role of Shōen Lords in Kujō Masamoto's Case" (*Reconsidering the Medieval Shōen System*, Iwata Shoin, 2007) / Republished in *Sengoku Period Territorial Power and Regional Society* (Iwata Shoin, 2009).</ref> The Blue Samurai continued to exist during the Edo period, where they also served as heralds when their lords entered the imperial palace. After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|en|Meiji Restoration|qid=Q8707|lt=Meiji Restoration|1=Meiji Restoration}}, those who had served as {{ill|jige officials|ja|地下官人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/jige officials|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 地下人|1=jige officials|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11424047}} or kuge retainers for at least three generations were recognized as {{ill|kanke shizoku|ja|官家士族|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kanke shizoku|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a category of {{ill|Shizoku|en|Shizoku|qid=Q3482406|lt=shizoku|1=Shizoku}}. Many of these officials, including the public officials of Tō-ji like {{ill|Ninagawa Shikata|ja|蜷川式胤|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninagawa Shikata|1=Ninagawa Noritane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q1430049}}, contributed to cultural heritage preservation and became government officials. Others took up priesthoods at shrines such as {{ill|Heian Shrine|en|Heian Shrine|qid=Q385141|lt=Heian Shrine|1=Heian Shrine}}, {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en|Atsuta Shrine|qid=Q482065|lt=Atsuta Shrine|1=Atsuta Shrine}}, and {{ill|Meiji Shrine|en|Meiji Shrine|qid=Q287165|lt=Meiji Shrine|1=Meiji Shrine}}. == Notable Blue Samurai == * {{ill|Shimokōbe Nagatsuru|ja|下河辺長流|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimokōbe Nagatsuru|1=Shimokōbe Chōryū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q6536415}} – Retainer of the {{ill|Sanjonishi family|ja|三条西家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanjonishi family|1=Sanjōnishi family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11355857}} during the early Edo period; a pioneer of {{ill|Kokugaku|en|Kokugaku|qid=Q855159|lt=kokugaku|1=Kokugaku}}. * {{ill|Honjō Munemasu|ja|本庄宗資|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Honjō Munemasu|1=Honjō Munesuke|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11520260}} – Early Edo period {{ill|Fudai daimyō|en|Fudai daimyō|qid=Q643640|lt=fudai daimyō|1=Fudai daimyō}} (lord of {{ill|Ashikaga Domain|en|Ashikaga Domain|qid=Q10863287|lt=Ashikaga Domain|1=Ashikaga Domain}}, later {{ill|Kasama Domain|en|Kasama Domain|qid=Q3034461|lt=Kasama Domain|1=Kasama Domain}}). Originally a retainer of the {{ill|Nijō family|en|Nijō family|qid=Q3065387|lt=Nijō family|1=Nijō family}}, he rose to prominence due to the patronage of {{ill|Keishō-in|ja|桂昌院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keishō-in|1=Keishōin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q5369704}}, the mother of {{ill|Tokugawa Tsunayoshi|en|Tokugawa Tsunayoshi|qid=Q313745|lt=Tokugawa Tsunayoshi|1=Tokugawa Tsunayoshi}}, who was his sister. * {{ill|Shimada Masatoki|ja|島田正辰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimada Masatoki|1=Shimada Masatatsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11476938}} (Sakon) – Retainer of the {{ill|Kujō family|en|Kujō family|qid=Q1791350|lt=Kujō family|1=Kujō family}} in the late Edo period; a key figure among the pro-shogunate faction. * {{ill|Iizumi Kinai|ja|飯泉喜内|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iizumi Kinai|1=Ii'izumi Kinai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11666441}} – Retainer of the {{ill|Sanjo family|ja|三条家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanjo family|1=Sanjō family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q4683560}} in the late Edo period; executed during the {{ill|Ansei Purge|en|Ansei Purge|qid=Q569487|lt=Ansei Purge|1=Ansei Purge}}. * {{ill|Kagawa Kageki|ja|香川景樹|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagawa Kageki|1=Kagawa Kageki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q1721095}} – Retainer of the {{ill|Tokudaiji family|en|Tokudaiji family|qid=Q11489589|lt=Tokudaiji family|1=Tokudaiji family}} in the late Edo period; a poet. Before serving Tokudaiji, he had served various kuge families. * {{ill|Ozaki Saburō|ja|尾崎三良|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ozaki Saburō|1=Saburō Ozaki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11465163}} – Retainer of the {{ill|Karasumaru family|ja|烏丸家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karasumaru family|1=Karasumaru family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q22126158}} and later the {{ill|Reizei family|en|Reizei family|qid=Q3065463|lt=Reizei family|1=Reizei family}}, before serving {{ill|Sanjō Sanetomi|en|Sanjō Sanetomi|qid=Q516646|lt=Sanjo Sanetomi|1=Sanjō Sanetomi}}. * {{ill|Kawashima family|ja|革島家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawashima family|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Retainers of the {{ill|Takatsukasa family|en|Takatsukasa family|qid=Q846799|lt=Takatsukasa family|1=Takatsukasa family}}; a branch of the {{ill|Seiwa Genji|en|Seiwa Genji|qid=Q2164991|lt=Seiwa Genji|1=Seiwa Genji}} {{ill|Satake clan|en|Satake clan|qid=Q2975363|lt=Satake clan|1=Satake clan}}. Served as {{ill|Hōkōshū|en|Hōkōshū|qid=Q11445492|lt=hōkōshū|1=Hōkōshū}} of the {{ill|Muromachi shogunate|ja|Ashikaga Shogunate|1=Ashikaga shogunate|2=ja|3=室町幕府|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muromachi shogunate|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q1154406}}. * {{ill|Chōshi family|ja|調子家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōshi family|qq=}} * {{ill|Nakakōji family|ja|中小路家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakakōji family|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == References == <references/> == Further Reading == * Yutaka Yamanaka, "Blue Samurai" (*Kokushi Daijiten* 1, Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1979, ISBN 978-4-642-00501-2) * Chikara Tamai, "Blue Samurai" (*Nihonshi Daijiten* 1, Heibonsha, 1993, ISBN 978-4-582-13101-7) * Toshiharu Yukawa, "On Blue Samurai Hōsho - A Form of Sengoku Period Kuge Documents" (*Studies in Japanese Cultural History: Festschrift for Arisaka Takamichi*, Dōhōsha Publishing, 1991, ISBN 978-4-8104-1067-9 / Republished in *Kuge Society and Shōen Economy in the Sengoku Period*, Zoku Gunsho Ruijū Kanseikai, 2005, ISBN 978-4-7971-0744-9, Part 3, Chapter 3) == Related Articles == * {{ill|Kuge Samurai Secret Record|ja|公家侍秘録|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuge Samurai Secret Record|1=Kuge Samurai Secret Record|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11392013}} – A manga by Rie Takase featuring a Blue Samurai as the protagonist in the late Edo period.
{{translated page|ja|青侍|version=87987629|comment=Imported full ja history}}
